goal: Build the local version 0.1 playable tank loop: one basic tank drives with arcade track controls, aims with mouse targeting, shows a green/red muzzle-to-target line trace, fires projectiles, registers hits, and applies simple health damage.

existing_foundation: TANK_MP_FEATURE_ROADMAP.json defines 0.1 MVP and post-0.1 features.
Tank scene exists with LowPolyBody, TurretPivot, Muzzle, and AimLine.
Current tank controller has WASD track steering, mouse turret aim, and partial green/red line-of-sight feedback.
Projectile scene exists but does not yet collide, report hits, or apply damage.
Terrain3D is installed and smoke-tested, but 0.1 can use a local test scene before full Terrain3D gameplay integration.

non_goals_for_v01: No armor deflection, ricochet, directional armor, or subsystem damage.
No rewrite of Online.gd or Steam lobby bootstrap.
No edits to unrelated dirty files.
stop_rules: Stop on secrets, tokens, or private IDs.
Stop on hotfile conflicts with user or agent edits.
Stop if Godot script checks fail and the fix is outside the slice.
Stop before commit or push if branch or remote state is unclear.

slices.outcome: Save this ABC plan and coordinate Alice/Bob/Charlie work.

Update operator-facing docs and feature roadmap wording so 0.1 explicitly requires mouse targeting, green/red line trace, shooting, hits, and simple health damage, while deflection/armor zones remain post-0.1.
Read-only audit of the best implementation path for VehicleStats, target dummy, projectile hit handling, and a local vehicle test scene using current Godot project patterns.
forbidden_files: No git staging, commits, pushes, or destructive commands.
verification: Read-only handoff with recommended exact file list, risks, and tests.

slices.outcome: Create VehicleStats resource and assign BasicTank stats through the Tank scene inspector/resource path. Move existing tank tuning values to stats while preserving current behavior.
Create a local VehicleTest scene with camera, light, ground, one playable tank, one target dummy, and one obstacle for blocked aim-line testing.
Make the 0.1 targeting contract robust: mouse target, independent turret yaw, muzzle-origin raycast, green clear line, red blocked line, and self-ignore.
verification: MCP runtime scene tree and node/property checks where available

slices.outcome: Implement local projectile firing from Muzzle, collision-based projectile impacts, target dummy health, and simple damage event structure that can later support armor zones and deflection.
verification: MCP runtime fire/hit smoke test if available

slices.outcome: Wire the playable tank to terrain sampling so local v0.1 terrain height and normals affect tank height and speed through a slope slowdown factor.
verification: MCP runtime slope smoke: flat-ish point factor=1.000; steeper point pos=(-10,12) angle=19.09 factor=0.845

alice_path.goal: Docs and roadmap wording match the v0.1 arcade targeting/shooting contract.
alice_path.completion: JSON validates, docs mention 0.1 vs post-0.1 clearly, no code edits.

bob_path.goal: Read-only technical handoff reduces implementation risk for stats/test/shooting.
bob_path.completion: Handoff lists exact files, recommended order, tests, and risks.

charlie_path.goal: Integrate 0.1 local gameplay loop, test, commit, and push.
charlie_path.completion: Focused Godot checks pass, only in-scope files staged, commit pushed to fuzzy/main.

go_language.go: Local VehicleTest scene proves one tank can drive, aim with green/red line trace, shoot, hit a target, and reduce health.
go_language.partial: Driving and targeting are solid, but shooting/hit damage is incomplete.
go_language.no_go: Tank targeting, scene loading, or projectile collision cannot be made stable within the current Godot scene foundation.
go_language.deferred: Armor deflection, hit zones, drones, spider tanks, crater damage, and multiplayer combat sync.
